title: Criteria-to-check mapping rule kind with Nyquist example
description:
Add a rule kind asserting that each criterion in a configured section maps to a verifiable check, and ship a Nyquist-style example ruleset built on it.

## Background

Methodologies like Nyquist validation require every hard requirement to have an automated verification path before work starts. The engine has no primitive linking criteria to checks; this stays methodology-agnostic by shipping the discipline as an example, not engine policy.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A rule kind asserts that each criterion (filterable by marker) in a configured section maps to a verifiable check (a configured gate or a declared check reference)
- [ ] An unmapped criterion yields a finding naming the criterion id and what is missing
- [ ] An example ruleset under docs/examples/ demonstrates requirement-to-check mapping end to end
- [ ] No methodology-specific names or behavior are added to engine code
